notes: ws['AM2'].value parsed as 'YYYY-YY' to get the FY start year; hardcoded fallback of 2026 if the cell can't be parsed. Independent of, and structurally different from, excel_extractor_rsp.py's own month/cum-column detection (COL_MAP_P18 dict + fixed AM column) — this extractor detects columns purely by scanning text headers instead of using a hardcoded letter map.

Row 3 is scanned for 3-letter month abbreviations (Apr..Mar) and the literal string 'Cum.' to locate the month column and the cumulative column, respectively. If report_month is supplied, the matching abbreviation's column is used directly; otherwise the LAST filled month column (scanned in reverse, Mar->Apr) with >5 numeric cells in rows 6-40 is chosen as an auto-detect heuristic.
